question: Quel champ exact est consommé par le moteur de matching en production locale actuelle ?
answer_short: ExtractedProfile.skills_uri (frozenset[str]) — comparé par intersection avec offer.skills_uri (list[str] dans le dict offre).

companion_reads.offer_skills_uri: apps/api/src/matching/matching_v1.py:368 — raw_uris = offer.get('skills_uri'); fallback to _map_offer_skills_to_uris(offer['skills']) at line 377 when missing

scoring_engine_entrypoints.score_offer: apps/api/src/matching/matching_v1.py:762 — called from inbox._score_offers and matching.match_profile
scoring_engine_entrypoints.match: apps/api/src/matching/matching_v1.py:861 — not currently used by /inbox; used by legacy /v1/match threshold-gated path

skills_uri_construction_truth_table.case: raw_profile['skills_uri'] is a non-empty list (typical case for CV-parsed profiles coming back from /profile/parse-*)
skills_uri_construction_truth_table.behavior: extract_profile USES THIS LIST VERBATIM (dedup only). SKILL_ALIASES expansion + map_skill are NOT executed.

skills_uri_construction_truth_table.case: raw_profile has no skills_uri or an empty list (plain JSON profiles, golden fixtures from Sprint 1/2)
skills_uri_construction_truth_table.behavior: extract_profile runs _expand_profile_skills via SKILL_ALIASES then map_skill(label, enable_fuzzy=False) per expanded label, then collapse_to_uris.
URIs appended (dedup) to the URI list built above.
build_effective_skills_uri(base_list, raw_profile) returns the frozenset assigned to ExtractedProfile.skills_uri.

flags_that_modify_scoring_input.effect_off: build_effective_skills_uri returns frozenset(base_list) — bit-for-bit identical to pre-Sprint-6 path.
flags_that_modify_scoring_input.effect_on: raw_profile['skills_uri_promoted'] URIs are unioned into the effective frozenset.

flags_that_modify_scoring_input.default: ON in dev-auto (ENV=dev/local OR ELEVIA_DEV_TOOLS=1 OR DEBUG=1), otherwise explicit 0/1
flags_that_modify_scoring_input.effect: Populates CVPipelineResult.domain_skills_active (display-only per module docstring). Does NOT inject into ExtractedProfile.skills_uri unless upstream also populates raw_profile['domain_uris'] (which the baseline path does NOT do today).
Applied to OFFER skills_uri (not profile). Removes high-frequency generic ESCO URIs before scoring.

which_cv_path_actually_feeds_scoring.verdict: The CV-brut pipeline (POST /profile/parse-file or POST /profile/parse-baseline) produces profile.skills_uri via profile.baseline_parser.run_baseline (i.e., strict_filter_skills + map_skill + collapse_to_uris on cv_text tokens). When the client round-trips that profile to /inbox or /v1/match, extract_profile SHORT-CIRCUITS the SKILL_ALIASES path and uses the pre-computed list verbatim (extractors.py:311-321).
which_cv_path_actually_feeds_scoring.implication: The audit in baseline/sprint2 (which describes SKILL_ALIASES as the main coverage mechanism) captures the behavior of the plain-JSON path (golden fixtures with no skills_uri pre-populated). It does NOT reflect the production-local CV-upload path where skills_uri is already present and alias expansion is not even called.
which_cv_path_actually_feeds_scoring.uncertainty: Whether real frontend calls to /inbox actually preserve skills_uri in the profile payload is a client-side contract that this cartography did NOT verify against the web app. It is possible that the frontend strips or rebuilds the profile before posting. Not traced here — flagged explicitly.

non_scoring_paths_clearly_tagged./profile/ingest_cv: DEV-only (ELEVIA_DEV_TOOLS=1). Legacy LLM CV extraction. Output shape is a different schema (CvExtractionResponse) — does not produce skills_uri. NOT on the scoring chain.
non_scoring_paths_clearly_tagged./apply-pack: Does its own string-level matched/missing computation (apply_pack.generator_v0.compute_matched_missing). Does NOT call MatchingEngine. Documentation / pack generation only.
non_scoring_paths_clearly_tagged./profile-understanding/session: ProfileUnderstandingService — parallel understanding product, not part of the matching scoring chain.
